Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about navigating the electric home ecosystem.

Privacy & Sim-Lab

Is my personal energy data stored?

No. We prioritize your privacy above all else. All simulations and calculations are performed locally within your browser, and no personal entries are ever saved or sent to a server.

Why are these tools free to use?

Our mission is to provide the "missing manual" for the electric home. We believe that providing transparent, educational tools helps the average homeowner make smarter decisions, save money, and reduce their environmental footprint.

How accurate are these simulations?

Our tools use verified industry benchmarks to provide realistic estimates. While they are perfect for planning and "what-if" scenarios, they are intended for educational guidance rather than professional engineering or electrical consultation.

EV Home Charging

What is the difference between Level 1 and Level 2 charging?

Level 1 uses a standard 120V household outlet and provides 3–5 miles of range per hour. Level 2 uses a 240V circuit (similar to a clothes dryer) and charges significantly faster, usually cutting charging time in half.

Will an EV charger overload my home's electrical panel?

It depends on your current capacity. Older homes with 100-amp panels may struggle if multiple high-load appliances are running. We recommend integrating a smart home energy management system to monitor your usage in real-time and automatically throttle your EV charger when other high-load appliances are active.

What is the best time of day to charge?

The most cost-effective time is during "off-peak" hours, typically late at night to early morning. Check with your utility provider for time-of-use (TOU) plans that can reduce your per-kWh costs by 30% to 50%.

Smart Energy & Home Systems

Can my car really power my home during an outage?

Only if your vehicle and charging setup support bi-directional charging (V2H/V2L). Most standard chargers cannot push power back into your home, so you'll need specific equipment to bridge that gap.

Is it worth installing an energy monitor?

Think of a smart monitor as a "fitness tracker" for your house. It reveals exactly which appliances are consuming your energy, helping you identify "vampire" or phantom loads that stay invisible on your monthly bill.

How do I get started as a complete beginner?

Start by exploring our Featured Guides to understand your home's baseline, then use our Sim-Lab to model your specific setup. You don't need to be an engineer to optimize your home; you just need to know how the pieces fit together.

No matching questions. Try a different word, or ask us directly below.

Still can't find the answer you're looking for?
